description: A Sicilian pastry made of crisp fried shells filled with a sweet creamy filling, traditionally based on ricotta. Cannoli are often finished with chocolate chips, candied fruit, chopped pistachios, or a dusting of sugar.
preparation_steps: Prepare and fry the pastry shells until crisp.
Drain and sweeten the ricotta filling, often with vanilla or citrus notes.
Pipe the filling into the shells shortly before serving.
Garnish with chocolate, nuts, or candied fruit.

nutrition.calories: Usually moderate to high per piece

health_advice.benefits: Offers some protein and calcium from ricotta.
health_advice.risks: High sugar and fried pastry make it an indulgent dessert.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Best enjoyed occasionally and filled just before serving to keep the shell crisp without becoming overly heavy.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional cannoli contain dairy and wheat. Gluten-free versions are possible only with specialty shells.

allergens: Tree nuts if pistachios are used
